name: Chiles En Nogada (Poblanos in Walnut Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fresh fruits: apple, pear, apricots, cubed
2 cups Walnut pieces, peeled and without the papery skin
1/4 piece Cream cheese, for flavor and consistency
Adorn: Red pomegranate seeds, parsley leaves
recipeInstructions:
The chiles should be large; if you cannot find large ones, use smaller ones but buy more. It's difficult to make the exact amount of chiles and stuffing.
Citron is dried citron made into sweetmeat; candied lemon. If unavailable, use other ingredients.
Walnuts must be peeled before using; place them in water on the refrigerator.
Prepare the stuffing:
1. In a saucepan, heat vegetable oil. Blend onion and garlic until smooth, add to hot oil and fry until brown (not black).
2. Add meats, cooking until they lose moisture and turn slightly brown. Stir in tomato puree, salt, pepper, and fruits; cook until dry. Let cool.
Prepare the sauce:
1. Mix all ingredients in a blender until smooth with a sauce consistency. Season with salt and cinnamon. The sauce will be cold over the stuffing chiles.
Serve:
1. Fill each chile with meat. Two serving options:
- Boil egg whites until firm, mix yolks and whites, cover chiles, fry briefly, cool, and drizzle with white sauce. Decorate with pomegranate seeds and parsley.
- Without eggs or oil, after filling, top with white sauce and a few pomegranate seeds for red color. Garnish with parsley.
Patricia Wriedt
